item.title: ‘Trying and Failing’ in Iran
item.description: What message is America’s performance in the Iran war sending the rest of the world? Elbridge Colby, under secretary of defense for policy, argues on “Interesting Times” that it’s one of American strength.

item.title: Canada: Neighbor or 51st State?
item.description: Is the United States actually going to annex Canada? On “Interesting Times,” the Opinion columnist Ross Douthat pushes the senior Trump administration official Elbridge Colby on just how real the threat of annexation is.

item.title: Carlos the Jackal’s Violent Chaos Endures
item.description: And Americans are nowhere near prepared to deal with it.

item.title: Putin’s Gamble Might Pay Off
item.description: No matter what the West does, Russia will not yield.

item.title: This Is How America Trounces China in the A.I. Race
item.description: Open-weight models have been the underlying strength of Chinese artificial intelligence strategy. America should take cues.

item.title: Gen Z Fell Out of Love With Work. I Don’t Blame Them.
item.description: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.

item.title: What’s in a Name? Depends Where You Are.
item.description: Place names change with time, depending on how the locals understand them.

item.title: Nothing Can Prevent the New Wildfires. We Have to Adapt.
item.description: Suppression is not enough.

item.title: Trump’s Tariff Runaround Defies the Courts and Common Sense
item.description: The administration will keep trying to run around rulings against import fees.

item.title: Mamdani’s Video Message About Netanyahu
item.description: Reader’s react to the New York mayor’s video criticizing the Israeli prime minister. Also: A.I. jobs for philosophers; a battle in the health care industry; fast walkers; pregnant bellies.

item.title: Tariffs Will Long Outlive Trump
item.description: We will be stuck with tariffs long after this president is out of office.

item.title: How Can Anyone Trust This Crew With Anything?
item.description: JD Vance isn’t going to save us. Neither is Pete Hegseth.

item.title: Trump’s Kiss of Death
item.description: On top of everything else, the president is inept.

item.title: Haiti Needs Stability, Not Mass Deportations
item.description: Deporting Haitian migrants will further destabilize a country in crisis.

item.title: The Second Home Tax Is a Win for New York
item.description: The recent debate over the new pied-à-terre tax in the city offers a useful case study.

item.title: How Trump and His Allies Can Corrupt the Midterms
item.description: Stop the steal before it starts.

item.title: The Grim Lessons of Israel’s War in Gaza
item.description: Israel has not been held accountable for its actions in Gaza. The nation and the individuals and countries enabling it to escape punishment may only benefit.

item.title: The Rise of Loneliness Influencers
item.description: “You don’t have to get married or be in a relationship to make a life for yourself,” Christine Emba says on “The Opinions,” arguing that the rise of loneliness influencers reflects a broader shift: Women no longer need relationships for economic or social survival.

item.title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
item.description: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”

item.title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
item.description: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”

item.title: ‘The Five-Star Weekend’ Reveres Its Middle-Aged Stars
item.description: Lately, the media loves to humiliate women in their 40s and 50s; this show makes middle age seem magical.

item.title: The Sex Recession Started With Millennials
item.description: The story of America’s sex recession doesn’t begin with Gen Z. Carter Sherman argues that millennials were already having less sex than previous generations — changing how we should think about what’s happening today.

item.title: Gen Z-ers Are Ditching Sex. They Might Be On to Something.
item.description: Is celibacy cool now?

item.title: Gen Z-ers Are Ditching Sex. They Might Be Onto Something.
item.description: Is celibacy cool now?

item.title: A Latino American Public Health Emergency
item.description: A reader responds to a guest essay about Latino Americans in crisis. Also: Dogs on summer vacation; chatting while waiting in line; the joy of dressing up.

item.title: Trump, Netanyahu and Putin Have All Blundered Into Dead-End Wars
item.description: The quick-fix fantasies of the world’s strongmen.

item.title: How Christopher Nolan Proved the ‘Odyssey’ Haters Wrong
item.description: The success of “The Odyssey,” and the potent political ideas at its core, only prove how tiresome the prerelease debate about “woke” really was.

item.title: A New Critique of Trumpism Goes Too Easy on Elites
item.description: Even under populism, elite failure is still a problem.

item.title: This Is a Bad Way to Address A.I. Job Loss. Of Course Trump Loves It.
item.description: The president has had the government take shares in all sorts of corporations. This approach would be particularly bad for the A.I. industry.

item.title: Maduro’s Case Is Due Back in Court. Prosecutors May Be in for a Big Surprise.
item.description: It’s a story a century old.

item.title: Trump’s New Insult Won’t Save Republicans
item.description: The president’s attempt to drum up a third red scare is certain to fail.

item.title: Call the Veep, Agent Mulder!
item.description: Have the “space Jews” come to get us at last?

item.title: JD Vance Is ‘Entirely Plastic’
item.description: JD Vance and Lindsey Graham both went from “never Trump” to MAGA loyalists. But the Opinion columnist Bret Stephens argues on “The Opinions” that the difference between them is that Graham held on to his core beliefs and got things done, while Vance has made a name for himself by endorsing “whatever suits a political moment.”

item.title: Maine Democrats Are Replacing an Oysterman With a Lumberjack. It Might Just Work.
item.description: For the state’s progressives, Graham Platner was tempting. Troy Jackson is tested.

item.title: Shining a Spotlight on the Atrocities in Sudan
item.description: Readers respond to an editorial, “The World Must Wake Up to the Horror in Sudan.” Also: Young people and guns; “gray divorce.”

item.title: Clothes Have All Started Looking the Same. Blame the Algorithm.
item.description: Fast fashion and mall brands used to look to the runways for inspiration. Now, much of their output has been run through an algorithm.

item.title: America Has 2 Political Parties but 8 Political Tribes
item.description: Think of our two major political parties as two coalitions.

item.title: I’m a Screenwriter. Hollywood Is in Trouble.
item.description: A scriptwriter’s lament about Hollywood’s decline

item.title: It’s No Longer So Much Fun to Be MAGA in Washington
item.description: It’s a vibe shift.

item.title: It’s No Longer So Much Fun to Be MAGA in Washington
item.description: It’s a vibe shift.

item.title: Enough Democratic Drama. North Carolina Has a Better, Boring Way.
item.description: There are important lessons in my state’s Senate race.

item.title: American Deaths in the Iran War
item.description: Readers react to the intensifying conflict. Also: Free speech and Israeli writers; treasuring immigrants.

item.title: This Is Why Our Fresh Produce Keeps Making Us Sick
item.description: Cyclospora outbreaks are just a small part of a much larger food safety crisis linked to fresh produce.

item.title: Capping the Price of Health Care Is a Mistake
item.description: A government bureaucrat doesn’t know how much your medicine or doctor’s visit should cost.

item.title: Don’t Let This Useless Debate Distract You, Democrats
item.description: A battle over idealized economic systems doesn’t help Democratic candidates.

item.title: A.I. Drones Are Coming. We Are Not Ready.
item.description: World leaders are marching us toward an uncertain era of new warfare — fast.

item.title: China’s A.I. Play Is Different From America’s
item.description: When great powers disagree about the nature of A.I.

item.title: A Billion Eyes. One Shared Experience.
item.description: The number of people who attend the World Cup is nothing compared with the sheer world-buckling biomass of those who experience it on a screen.

item.title: A Billion Eyes. One Shared Experience.
item.description: The number of people who attend the World Cup is nothing compared with the sheer world-buckling biomass of those who experience it on a screen.

item.title: Social Media Hacked Our Attention. A.I. Scares Me More.
item.description: A.I. plays on our heartstrings, manipulating the systems in our brains that bond us to other people.

item.title: American Jews in Difficult Times
item.description: Readers respond to “The Liberal American Jew’s Dilemma,” an Opinion guest essay by Nicholas Lemann. Also: Madonna’s message; death of a laborer.

item.title: ‘The Boys’ Is the Biggest Snub of the Emmys
item.description: “The Boys” was supersnubbed.

item.title: What Do the Words ‘Gay’ and ‘Queer’ Really Mean?
item.description: Readers respond to a guest essay about the labels used to describe those in the L.G.B.T.Q. community.

item.title: Low-T, High Chair
item.description: Where are Hegseth’s hormones when Trump needs them?

item.title: An Eye-Opening Visit to the ‘Little House’ of My Childhood
item.description: Laura Ingalls Wilder and the myth of the American West revisited.

item.title: My Son Was Killed by ICE. I Want Accountability.
item.description: I deserve accountability and transparency, and so does the public.

item.title: How Trump Abused the Power to Declassify America’s Secrets
item.description: The president is manipulating the intelligence community into a tool of misinformation.

item.title: The Housing Crisis Is Also a Retirement Crisis
item.description: A home has stopped being just a place to live — it has also become an asset.

item.title: Can Democrats Finally Understand the Latino Voter in an Era of Mass Deportations?
item.description: For decades, Democrats have misunderstood Latino voters. Trump’s deportation campaign offers a chance for them to get it right.

item.title: When I Sued Roger Ailes, #MeToo Was Just Starting. Now It’s the Law of the Land.
item.description: And we’re still fighting.

item.title: The King of the North, Andy Burnham, Won’t Save Britain
item.description: For the country, Andy Burnham looks a lot like more of the same.

item.title: The World Must Not Forget About the Genocide in Sudan
item.description: The longer the fighting drags on, the greater the chance that regional instability will spread.

item.title: The Lo-Fi Way I Broke My Scrolling Habit
item.description: I tried many remedies to break my social media habit. A homemade hack worked best.

item.title: Lindsey Graham Knew Better
item.description: He could see the obvious truth that Trump was a malign and corrosive force in American politics — that is, until Trump won the White House.

item.title: We’re Living in a Tick Nightmare. It’s Time to Go to War.
item.description: With ticks, we’ve barely begun to fight back. The longer we wait, the more territory they will claim across both our geographic and psychic maps.

item.title: The World Is Cutting Ties With America. It’s Already Costing Us.
item.description: When other countries cut ties, Americans pay.

item.title: Serena Williams Is Back. Thank Goodness.
item.description: Until Serena Williams announced her return to tennis at 44, I never realized how much I needed her.

item.title: In Sudan, a Preventable Atrocity
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of people in Sudan are bracing for their city to be overrun by a militia notorious for committing atrocities.

item.title: More Than One Way to Fix Social Security
item.description: Readers respond to two guest essays about how to cover the shortfall in the program’s funding before it’s too late.
